name: Shopware Store API
what_it_does: Open-source PHP ecommerce platform with dual API — Store API for headless storefronts and Admin API for merchant automation. Strong in DACH market and mid-market headless commerce.
use_cases: Building headless storefronts using Store API with any frontend framework
Automating product catalog management and pricing via Admin API
Syncing inventory, orders, and customer data with ERP systems
Managing promotions, discounts, and rule-based pricing programmatically
Triggering order fulfillment workflows via webhooks
not_for: Simple small-business stores where Shopify is easier to operate
Teams without PHP/Symfony expertise for self-hosted deployments
Pure marketplace platforms (Shopware is single-seller focused)
best_when: An agent manages a headless mid-market ecommerce operation in the DACH region or needs open-source ecommerce with full API access.
avoid_when: You need a fully managed SaaS ecommerce with minimal operational overhead — use Shopify instead.

auth.notes: Admin API uses OAuth2 client credentials with granular ACL scopes. Store API uses sw-access-key header (sales channel API key). Integration users can have fine-grained permissions per resource.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community Edition: Apache 2.0, self-hosted, fully free
pricing.notes: Shopware 6 Community Edition is free open-source. Commercial cloud plans add managed hosting, support, and enterprise features. Strong ecosystem of German system integrators.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no built-in rate limiting; Cloud: throttled per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec available for Admin API via Stoplight. Store API also documented. Error responses are JSON with violation arrays for validation failures. PHP stack traces can appear in dev mode.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Product and category creation can produce duplicates — no built-in idempotency keys. Order status transitions are idempotent. Use external ID fields to detect duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Store API and Admin API are separate — different base URLs and auth methods
Sales channel context required for all Store API calls — agents need to track channel IDs
Indexing is async — after bulk product changes, search index may lag
Flow/rule engine is powerful but complex — testing required before automation

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 with fine-grained ACL scopes for Admin API. Store API keys are sales-channel scoped. GDPR-compliant data handling. Open-source codebase is auditable. Regular security advisories published.
